questions.question: In a table one can have:
Display the employees that have the same manager as the manager of their department.

answers.text: select * from employees e, departments d where e.department_id = d.department_id and e.manager_id = d.manager_id;
select * from employees natural join departments;
select * from employees e join departments d using (manager_id, department_id);
select * from employees e, departments d where e.manager_id = d.manager_id;

questions.question: Choose the correct answers regarding scalability:

answers.text: Vertical scalability means scaling by adding more power (CPU, RAM) to an existing machine
Horizontal scalability means scaling by adding more power (CPU, RAM) to an existing machine
Vertical scalability is applied for relational DBMSs
Horizontal scalability is applied for SQL DBMSs
Horizontal scalability is applied for NoSQL DBMSs

questions.question: The characteristics of the relational model:

answers.text: It made the transition to a new generation (the second).
The model implementation is made at logical level by attributes with key roles.
It was proposed by IBM and revolutionized the representation of data in the database.
The model is simple, has a solid theoretical foundation being based on the set theory (assemblies) and mathematical logic.
All types of highly complex data structures from different fields can be represented using this model.

questions.question: The SELECT statement:

answers.text: includes the mandatory clauses FROM and WHERE
contains the optional clauses GROUP BY and HAVING
is sensitive to uppercase/lowercase for the keywords
is part of the DDL (Data Definition Language) category

questions.question: The query 'SELECT MIN (salary)... WHERE e.department_id = d.department_id (+) ... UNION ... WHERE e.department_id (+)= d.department_id' is equivalent to:

answers.text: SELECT MIN (salary) AS min_salary, d.department_id, d.department_name FROM employees e inner join departments d on e.department_id = d.department_id GROUP BY d.department_id, d.department_name;

SELECT MIN (salary) AS "min_salary", d.department_id, d.department_name FROM employees e, departments d WHERE e.department_id(+) = d.department_id and e.department_id = d.department_id (+) GROUP BY d.department_id, d.department_name;
SELECT MIN (salary) AS min_salary, d.department_id, d.department_name FROM employees e full outer join departments d on e.department_id = d.department_id GROUP BY d.department_id, d.department_name;
SELECT MIN (salary) AS min_salary, d.department_id, d.department_name FROM employees e, departments d WHERE e.department_id(+) = d.department_id (+) GROUP BY d.department_id, d.department_name;

questions.question: The dynamic analysis of a database system:

answers.text: Has as result the data flow diagram

questions.question: Choose the correct answer(s) regarding Normal Forms:

answers.text: A table is NF4 if and only if is NF3 and is not containing two or more multivalue dependencies.
A table is in the 2nd NF if all its attributes contain basic values (nondecomposable) meaning that each tuple must not have group or repetitive data.
A table in NF2 still has a number of update anomalies due any multi-value dependencies.
A table in NF2 still has a number of update anomalies due to any transitive dependencies.
A table is in the 2nd NF if and only if is in NF1 and each non-key attribute of the table is fully functionally dependent on the key.
